### Post by schnauzer93 on 2008-04-12
When I try to play games such as Urban Terror and Wolfenstein: Enemy Territory, the graphics look very... sketchy, with dark areas, flashing players, etc.

My xorg.conf looks like this:
```

```

Direct rendering is disabled, which is what I think is causing my difficulties.

fglrxinfo output:
```

display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: ATI Radeon X1200 Series
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6473 (8.37.6)
```

I have the restricted drivers installed and enabled. However, when I boot into failsafe GNOME, Direct Rendering switches to "Yes". Also, when I un-install the xserver-xgl package, direct rendering works well when I boot normally Unfortunately,, this disables Compiz. Is there any way I can have both Compiz and the ability to play games? Turnig off desktop effects has no impact on graphics performance; I have to log out and log into failsafe GNOME in order for graphics to be displayed properly.

---

### Post by wylfing on 2008-04-12
You're probably right that not having direct rendering enabled is causing your problems. The fglrxinfo output looks fine.

I don't really know the ins and outs of it, but xserver-xgl seems to bork direct rendering on a lot of systems. If you have an nVidia graphics card, you can get all the fancy desktop effects just using xserver-xorg. But if you have an ATI card, my understanding is that you need to use xserver-xgl in order to get those effects. So your choice is probably going to be "Do I want fancy desktop effects or do I want games?" 

If you choose games, then here's what you'll have to do:
[LIST=1]
[*]Open up a terminal.
[*][FONT="Courier New"]sudo apt-get remove xserver-xgl[/FONT]
[*][FONT="Courier New"]sudo apt-get --reinstall install xserver-xorg[/FONT]
[*]Turn off desktop effects (System > Preferences > Appearance).
[*]Reboot.
[/LIST]

When you get back up, check to see if direct rendering is enabled.

---

### Post by schnauzer93 on 2008-04-12
Ah yes, that fixes it. It's too bad though... I liked Compiz... :(

EDIT: Unless there is a way to add an option in the "sessions" dialog where I can choose to load/not load xserver-xgl?

EDIT 2: Nevermind, I went back to Compiz. I realized that time spent playing games < time spent spinning the desktop cube. :)
